entry.title: Re: Does project release workflow impact data migration o...
entry.content:
Dear @AIDream,

SAP does not explicitly document that Migration Cockpit bypasses the project release workflow. However based on SAP standard documentation the migration object is intended for the initial creation of Enterprise Projects, while the workflow is intended for the project release step. Therefore the workflow should normally not interrupt migration unless the migration or a follow-up action sets the project into a release-relevant processing status

What SAP does state is:

  • migration objects are for initial migration, meaning you create data with them and SAP also states that Migration Cockpit cannot be used to update data for Enterprise Project corrections/updates SAP points to the API instead
  • the Enterprise Project workflow is described as a workflow for releasing a project, with approvers approving/rejecting/sending back the release
  • in the Enterprise Project API changing the processing status is a separate operation (ChangeEntProjProcgStatus) which shows that status/release handling is treated separately from simple project creation

Summary:

Migrate legacy projects in a non-released status then test one sample release in QAS before mass load. That is the safest statement supported by SAP documentation

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3/2602.500/en-US/FSD_CE2602.pdf

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/0bfec05444d040078447b06fa2e1fe43.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: BiWhy – Why a Diagnostics Platform Is Needed
entry.content:
Disclaimer:
BiWhy is available for free to everyone at biwhy.net.

BiWhy – Motivation and Principles

A diagnostics platform is needed to simplify and automate diagnostics, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis.

In many enterprise environments, the required diagnostic data exists, but it is scattered across logs, metrics, configuration, files, databases, and multiple tools. As a result, root cause analysis is often slower, more manual, and more dependent on individual experience than it should be.

This article outlines some supportability principles for enterprise software and the practical approach behind BiWhy, where I also tried to apply them. These principles help make software easier to operate, troubleshoot, and improve.

The motivations and design approach behind BiWhy – principles I also tried to apply within BiWhy itself

Software supportability
Some of the principles behind supportable software include the following:

  • Measure everything.
  • Log effectively.
    • Log everything important.
    • Support on-the-fly verbosity configuration.
    • Use centralized configuration wherever possible.
    • Cut noise.
      • Remove worthless log entries.
      • Maintain zero error tolerance – a healthy system should be clear of error and exception logs. It is either healthy or it has errors; it cannot be both.
      • There should not be errors that can simply be ignored. Either fix them or do not mark them as errors.
      • Provide easy log filtering at the point of generation as a practical workaround for the two issues above.
  • Make key information easily available, as simply as possible, ideally in one click.
    • Measures
    • Logs
    • Documentation
  • Automate or semi-automate repeatable problem detection and troubleshooting.
  • Provide a convenient UI to analyze all of the above.
  • Ensure that non-trivial configurations are guided and easily troubleshootable at each step.

Good supportability means

  • Problems and performance can be addressed and optimized starting from the lowest level.
    • End users
    • Customer report developers
    • System administrators
    • Vendor support layers
    • Vendor developers

This reduces pressure on higher-tier support layers and can save significant support effort every day.

Part of those resources can then be redirected to further automation and supportability improvement.

  • Overall, it helps create best-in-class software in terms of functionality richness, performance, robustness, and total cost of ownership.
    • Early error detection, including regressions, facilitates new feature development and problem correction.
    • Performance optimization at all levels leads to broader overall performance gains.

Example

SAP’s success with ABAP systems was, to a great extent in my view, supported by open architecture: logs lead to code, the code is easy to debug and measure, and customers can see what is going on and participate in development.

BusinessObjects addresses supportability in many areas, but it still has important gaps.

BiWhy was started with the idea of helping fill those gaps.

entry.title: IOS and Android Version Details for SAP Mobile Start Users
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

We are using SAP BTP Work Zone, standard edition, and SAP Mobile Start. We would like to know whether it is possible to view the iOS and Android versions of users who are logged in to SAP Mobile Start. Kindly let us know if any such feature is available in SAP Mobile Start.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: IOS and Android Version Details for SAP Mobile Start...
entry.content:
Regarding your query, please notice that there is no standard feature in SAP Mobile Start to directly view individual user data such as iOS/Android versions.

As an alternative, you could use Mobile Services User Data charts: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/usage/admin/user-data.html

For example, with SAP BTP Mobile Services analytics, you can access aggregated device information such as: 

- Platform (iOS / Android)

- Platform version (OS version)

- Device model

- App version

These are available in analytics dashboards (User Data / Sessions) and can be filtered/reporting-oriented.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, March 19th, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS 

ABAP Developer Challenge – halfway point! 

  • ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/abap-developer-challenge-joule-for-developers-using-basic-trial/ba-p/14336191 

SAP Insider Las Vegas 

  • Event website https://vegas.sapinsider.org/  

Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition 

  • Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-sap-integration-suite-enhanced-edition/ba-p/14346638  

CAP Feb 2026 release 

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/2026/feb26  

Feedback for the btp CLI and Terraform providers 

  • Post with links to both surveys https://www.linkedin.com/posts/christian-lechner-inthecloud_do-you-use-the-btp-cli-andor-terraform-activity-7435231498376482816-rjWW/  

DYK #8 Orchestration Client in SAP Cloud SDK for AI 

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI – Chat Completion documentation https://sap.github.io/ai-sdk/docs/js/orchestration/chat-completion  

CHAPTER TITLES  

00:00 Intro 
00:07 ABAP Developer Challenge – halfway point! 
00:39 SAP Insider Las Vegas 
01:59 Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition  
03:10 CAP Feb 2026 release 
04:58 Feedback for the btp CLI and Terraform providers  
05:56 DYK #8 Orchestration Client in SAP Cloud SDK for AI 

TRANSCRIPT

This is the SAP Developer News for March 19th, 2026.

[Sheena]
Hello, everyone. We are halfway through the March Developer Challenge on Joule
ABAP AI capabilities. And we would like to thank all of you for the amazing
responses we have been receiving for the past two weeks. As you might know,
week three is already live and we are focusing on RAP predict business logic to
create a sample code by passing a simple prompt. We have one more week
remaining and we are excited to hear even more great responses. Till then, stay
Jouled!

[Rich and Witalij]
Hey folks, it's Rich Heilman and Witalij here in Las Vegas for SAP Insider
2026. Today we did a two-hour hands-on session on Joule Studio, which was good.
Lots of good questions and tomorrow we're going to repeat that and we're going
to do a Joule for Developers in SAP Build and a Joule for Developers ABAP
edition as well where we'll show everyone how to use Joule to speed up their
development process. And you did a hands-on session this morning. Yes and I did
a session on how to use SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine Capabilities to build the
Joule augmented generation. Lots of people are talking about agents right now
but a lot of customers are doing RAG based AI applications so it was good to
refresh this content and as well to mention that we still keep investing in
some of the capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine like for example the
very recent new encoder model. Cool very cool yeah so we got a couple more
hands-on sessions and I think we're gonna get out here and get on to the next
one. So, bye for now.

[Antonio]
Hola, SAP developers. There is a new edition available for SAP Integration
Suite, which includes lots of goodies. This is the new Enhanced Edition. From
an AI perspective, it includes features that were only previously available in
the Premium Edition. For example, iFlow generation using natural language,
script optimization to analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript
scripts, API anomaly detection, and API traffic prediction to help you manage
your APIs. Apart from that, you also get access to the entire suite of
integration capabilities, an advanced event mesh broker for your event-driven
architecture scenarios, SAP Document AI to turn unstructured data into
structured data, the alert notification service for monitoring, transport
management service to move artifacts across landscapes without manual exports.
So, lots of stuff. Make sure to check out Tricia's blog post on SAP Community
to get all the details. Hasta luego. Hello, everyone.

[Ajay]
The February release of Capire documentation (*CAP) is out. First, we have an
exciting update. Live queries in the documentation. Yes, you have heard that
right. You can now run CDS queries directly in your browser. You can view the
query results along with the corresponding SQL statements. You can also edit
the query by typing in the box, making it your own personal playground to
experiment with, and no setups required. Coming to Node.js updates. Parallel
GET calls are now supported in the OData $batch request. For draft-enabled
entities, calculated elements can now be reliably used for values display in
the UI or for information in the UI behavior. Starting with @cap-js/sqlite
version Node.js SQL implementation by setting cds.requires.db.driver to node.
Coming to Java updates, important changes include using a reference as a value
for substring prefix or suffix in the contains startsWith or endsWith functions
is now rejected. Performance improvements have been made for requests related
to Fiori Draft list report "All" filter, deletion of inactive drafts during the
draft activation, and hierarchical selects. Tooling updates includes a new
query mode in cds REPL is available via the .ql command. CAP now supports
version 10 of ESLint. And for the Cloud Foundry, cds up can now be adjusted
using MTA extensions. For more details, visit the Capire documentation. Links
will be included in the description.

[DJ]
If you use the SAP Business Technology Platform, and more specifically, if
you're using the btp CLI or the Terraform providers to help you manage your
resources there, then your feedback is sought. The team has a couple of
feedback surveys open right now, running through April as well, so it's still a
little bit of time. One feedback survey for the btp CLI and the other for the
Terraform providers. This is a great opportunity for you to provide your
opinions and share with the team how you're using the tools and also where you
see room for improvement. Links to both surveys, which, as I mentioned, are
running sort of through April, are in the description. After this news episode
finishes, why not grab a coffee and head over and give your opinion right now.
Thanks.

[Kevin]
Did you know that you can configure an AI orchestration flow in AI Launchpad
and use it to configure the orchestration client from the SAP Cloud SDK for AI
within a CAP project? It allows you to configure templating, content filtering,
data masking, grounding, and more. You can download the configuration and read
it from file into your orchestration client or directly paste the JSON string
from AI Launchpad into your project. At any point, you can upload the
configuration back to AI Launchpad to make changes or directly change the
config inline of your project.

entry.content:
Thanks Nata for a detailed explanation of the Feature Flag Service. 

It seems a nice strategy to use to control the release of features and functionalities on the fly without reverting code.

I do have a couple of questions:

  1. The example covers the functionality very well when its microservices involved. How would this work if there is UI involved or other interfaces that the service could interact with e.g. SAP BPA. How would you control UI elements visibility or process tasks from executing based on the feature flag ?

  3. How would this work with a multi-tenant SaaS application ? Can we control the feature flags based on customers ?

  Would definitely love a deep-dive session looking at the complete app with UI, microservices, external APIs, interfaces etc. involved. Thanks once again for the lovely writeup.

Regards,

Melwyn

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks
entry.content:
**Updated March 2026**

Welcome to our blog series focused on the SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity (BDC) Unit Estimator for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks.

This guide provides a comprehensive, step-by-step approach to help you estimate the capacity units (CU) needed for your business case (before purchasing) or how you can distribute them across your solution components (after signing a contract) for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks.

This post belongs to a blog series with 5 parts: 

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks (this post)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP Datasphere (coming soon)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP Databricks (coming soon)

What’s SAP BDC Connect? It is a service available within SAP BDC that enables secure, bidirectional, zero-copy sharing of data products between SAP BDC catalog and partner systems.

This service is not designed for replication scenarios. For these cases, organizations need to consider Premium Outbound Integration (POI) in SAP Datasphere.

As of February 2026, SAP BDC Connect supports integration exclusively with Databricks across AWS, GCP, and Azure environments. Additional integrations (including Google BigQuery, Snowflake, and Microsoft Fabric) are planned for release by the end of the year.

For SAP BDC Connect for Databricks, consumption is measured in Capacity Units (CU). The total consumption varies based on: A) Inter-Region Data Transfer and B) Processing Requests.

1. Inter-Region Data Transfer: Data volume shared to external data platforms in GB (Gigabyte). It only applies when the “region” is different. When we say “region”, please understand as hyperscaler + geographic region (for example: AWS US East or US10). If we have a cross-hyperscaler or cross-region scenario, this metric is considered. Check more details on the table below.

 

Scenario

Databricks  “Region”

BDC Connect “Region”

Is considered Inter-Region?

Cross-hyperscaler (different hyperscaler, same region)

AWS EU10

Azure EU20

Yes

Cross-region (same hyperscaler, different region)

AWS CA10

AWS EU10

Yes

Ideal (same hyperscaler and region)

AWS US10

AWS US10

No

2. Processing Requests: Blocks of API calls for data requests (1 block = 2500 API calls).

Example: In a cross-region scenario, a customer with a total data volume of 500 GB and 1,000 blocks of API calls would consume 147.50 Capacity Units (CU).

📌  Want to estimate how many CU you need? No problem! Below, you can find a step-by-step:

1. Open SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator.

2. Select your "Hyperscaler" and "Data Center".

3. Click "Add" and double-click "SAP Business Data Cloud Connect".

4. Enter the number of Inter-Region Data Transfer and Processing Requests. In this example, we use 500 GB of data volume and 1,000 blocks of APIs. 

5. The final CU / Month amount will appear on the left. 

Image 01

Image 02

👉  If you plan to provision SAP BDC Connect for Databricks, please follow this guidance. Important: Always provision the service in the same region and hyperscaler as your SAP BDC Cockpit!

📈  If you want to validate your actual consumption for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks, please check this step-by-step. 

Sources:

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Service Description Guide
  • Provisioning SAP Business Data Cloud Connect
  • Data Center Availability in SAP Business Data Cloud

entry.title: Re: Tracking Capacity Unit Usage for SAC and SAP Databricks
entry.content: Hello, you can use this article as reference to check consumption across BDC components - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-check-consumption-for-sap-business-data-cloud-bdc-and-its-components/ba-p/14257413

entry.title: Re: Minimum CU for Datasphere with BDC Connector?
entry.content: Yes, a Datasphere tenant is required, and it needs to have object store enabled, since it is this feature that enable the creation of custom data products, which we share through BDC and then Databricks. You need to have a Datasphere tenant with a minimum of 4151 Capacity Units (CU).

entry.title: What’s New, Now Relevant: Introducing Release Center
entry.content:
Every six months, SAP SuccessFactors releases a package of innovation: new features, enhancements, and capabilities that drive your HR practice forward. Change comes at a cost, however, and we know it. You have six weeks to understand, adopt, and test everything in the package. It’s a lot to manage in a short time. You told us. We heard you.

Through your feedback, SAP SuccessFactors designed a new experience to alleviate the burden of change management. It helps you find the new features, enhancements, and capabilities that matter to you, your tenant, and your HR practice.

We call it Release Center.

The Limits of Today’s Release Experience

The What’s New Viewer runs on the SAP Help Portal. It hosts the list of all changes across SAP SuccessFactors. But because it runs outside of SAP SuccessFactors, it can’t know your tenant, so when you open the page, it shows you all changes to all SAP SuccessFactors products. It can’t intelligently filter itself what matters for your tenant:

  • When you first open the page, you see all changes, not just those that apply to you. To see the changes that apply to your HR practice and tenant, you use your deep product knowledge to filter the list.
  • It’s hard to personalize the list for your HR Practice and tenant, and it’s even harder to share it with your team. Often, a spreadsheet is the best answer, but it’s not dynamically updated when we must update an article.
  • SAP SuccessFactors can’t build leading practices into a workflow experience.

Importantly, the What’s New Viewer on the Help Portal isn’t going away. It still runs the subscription service, it’s still the easiest way to share content publicly, and it’s embedded with the rest of SAP’s products.

Release Center: Breaking Through the Limits

Release Center is a new experience in the Admin Center. It starts our transformation from the current SAP Portal experience to guided, intelligent, and collaborative experiences.

We’re proud to focus on our Administrators’ experiences and give you tools to make your work faster, easier, and more personalized.

Release Center is Tenant Aware

Release Center automatically understands your enabled products and modules and your configured features so that it can surface the release highlights that matter to your tenant.

Release Center has Personalized Impact Intelligence

Not all updates impact your tenant or your HR practice equally. Release Center separates what is critical for your usage of SAP SuccessFactors because it knows how you use it. You can focus critical issues that require immediate attention for your configuration and leave lower priority changes for later.

Release Center Handles End-to-End Release Management

For the first time, release change management is a managed workflow inside SAP SuccessFactors. You can:

  • Assign release highlights to specific administrators
  • Track progress on individual articles with statuses like In Progress or Completed
  • Collaborate with others by adding comments and discussions to articles
  • Reduce dependency on offline tools like spreadsheets

Release Center is Designed with You and for You

Release Center isn’t just another tool. It’s the result of extensive collaboration with administrators and partners. We listened to you when you told us:

  • “Help me focus on what matters”
  • “Show me impact, not just information”
  • “Give me a way to manage releases with my team”

Our first version is built directly from that feedback, and it’s just the beginning. We can’t wait to hear what you think of Release Center and how we can relentlessly improve it.

What’s Next

We are actively investing in:

  • Deeper intelligence and recommendations
  • Expanded collaboration capabilities
  • Enhanced analytics and reporting
  • Broader coverage across the suite

Your feedback is critical. It shapes what we do next.

Starting your Journey with us in Release Center

We invite you to explore Release Center in the 1H 2026 release, integrate it into your release processes, and share your feedback through Influence channels and surveys.

Because a release process shouldn’t just inform you—they should empower you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere – Replication Flow – Loading delta data from SAP S/4HANA – Q & A Session
entry.content:
Below Answer is not correct and this option is not available through data builder only possible through database.                             Can we Export the result of this preview to Excel?
A:
Yes — in SAP Datasphere, you can export preview data to a CSV file which can then be opened in Microsoft Excel. Simply open the Data Preview in the Data Builder or View Editor, then click the Export button (download icon) to download the data as a CSV file

entry.title: Fiori Launchpad (WEBGUI) Default Download Path
entry.content:
Currently, the default Download Path of Fiori App (WEBGUI) is "Z:\".    How could to change it to some other download path?   


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Launchpad (WEBGUI) Default Download Path
entry.content:
Hello,

As per 2636752 - WebGUI File Browser: How to download files to C:\ instead of Z:\

Only Z:\ drive is available as download path for WEBGUI, this is expected behavior.

Meanwhile, with the fix of Note 3092845 Webgui FSM: download all files also to the native file system, all files that are downloaded to the z drive are also downloaded to the native file system.

The native file system refers to the download path defined in browser settings. See also KBA 3231710 - WebGUI File Browser: How to change download path while downloading to local drive (native file system)

It is also possible to export files to the native file system manually. Refer to "File Export" described in KBA 2249454 - ITS Up/Down: Using the WEBGUI File Browser.

entry.title: How do you gracefully withdraw CDs for managed landscapes that are already decommissioned?
entry.content:
We are running Solution Manager 7.2 (SP14).  A handful of landscapes previously managed by ChaRM were recently decommissioned and now no longer exist.  Unfortunately, the team that performed the decommissioning activities did not close all of the open change documents for these landscapes.  I want to gracefully withdraw all of the open CDs for these landscapes.  Of course, I can't withdraw any CDs for these landscapes in the ChaRM webUI if they had transport requests assigned.

I could use program CRM_SOCM_SERVICE_REPORT to unconditionally withdraw these CDs, but I would like to avoid that if possible.  I'd like to somehow remove the TRs from the CDs and withdraw them from the webUI.  I can't use a program like /TMWFLOW/TRANSPORT_DELETE to remove the TRs from the CDs because the RFCs can't communicate with systems that no longer exist.

Is there any possibility to accomplish this or is my only option to unconditionally withdraw the CDs?

entry.title: Re: Question About Certification Requirement for Access t...
entry.content:
Hi @Takuma_Masushige,

If you are a customer seeking access to Provisioning, please note that it is not granted to customers, even if you hold certifications. Provisioning is a shared backend across all SuccessFactors modules though relevant module certification is required. Please refer to the KBA 2273365 for more details.
